document.heading: Installment Payment Plans – Property Taxes in Arrears – Residential Property
An Ordinance Concerning Installment Payment Plans – Property Taxes in Arrears – Residential Property

container.heading: Installment Payments for Taxes in Arrears

para.heading: In general.
para.text: In this subtitle, the following terms have the meanings indicated.

para.heading: Department.
para.text: "Department" means the Baltimore City Department of Finance.

para.heading: Director.
para.text: "Director" means the Baltimore City Director of Finance or the Director's designee.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Dwelling" means:
a building that is used, in whole or in part, for residential occupancy; and
the lot or curtilage on which the building is erected.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Dwelling" includes:
a condominium unit;
an apartment in a cooperative apartment corporation; and
a part of real property used other than primarily for residential purposes, if the real property is used as a principal residence by an individual who has a legal interest in the real property.

para.heading: Eligible property.
para.text: "Eligible property" means any property that has a dwelling.

para.heading: Property.
para.text: "Property" means any unit of real property that is subject to the real property tax of Baltimore City.

para.heading: Establishment.
para.text: The Director shall establish an installment payment program:
in which any person with a legal interest in an eligible property may enroll; and
that allows any person with a legal interest in an eligible property to:
make payment of taxes in arrears on an eligible property; and
make payment of any fee or charge of any kind that is included on a property tax bill for that eligible property.

para.heading: Determination of compliance.
para.text: A person is not in compliance with the terms of an installment payment plan if the minimum payment on that installment payment plan is not made for a period of 90 days after the date the payment is due.

para.heading: Prohibition against action if in compliance.
para.text: If a person is in compliance with the terms of an installment payment plan under this section, the Director may not take action under Title 14, Subtitle 8 of the State Tax-Property Article {"Collection"} to collect any property taxes in arrears that are included in the installment payment plan.

para.heading: Remedies available if not in compliance.
para.text: If a person is not in compliance with the terms of an installment payment plan, the Director may:
terminate the person's installment payment plan; and
take action under Title 14, Subtitle 8 of the State Tax-Property Article {"Collection"} to collect the property taxes in arrears that were included in the installment payment plan.

para.heading: Notice.
para.text: Each offer of an installment payment plan shall:
include the availability of an installment payment plan;
state the minimum terms of the plan that would be acceptable to the Director;
explain any alternate terms that may be available;
state the date by which the Department must be contacted to enter into an installment payment plan;
indicate the name and telephone number of a Department representative that a person may call to discuss the installment payment program; and
state what action the Department may take if the person does not enter into an installment payment plan by the date specified in paragraph (4) of this subsection.

para.heading: Terms of plan.
para.text: An installment payment plan shall set forth:
the total amount and dates of the delinquent charges covered by the plan;
the total value of the plan, if different than the amount in paragraph (1) of this subsection;
the time period over which the total amount shall be paid;
the number, due date, and amount of each installment payment due under the plan; and
whether invoices will be sent by mail or electronic means.

para.heading: Plan statement.
para.text: The Department shall send to a person with an installment payment plan a statement that provides 30 days' notice of each installment payment due under the plan.

para.heading: Annual report.
para.text: On or before July 1 of each year, the Department shall provide to the Mayor and City Council a report that contains the following information:
the total number of persons enrolled in an active installment payment plan and the average amount of arrearage for all plans;
the total number of persons that entered into an installment payment plan in the prior fiscal year and the average amount of arrearage;
the number of persons enrolled in an installment payment plan that are not in compliance with the plan and the average amount of arrearage;
a summary of the resources that are available to assist a person that is not in compliance with an installment payment plan;
a summary of the outreach conducted by the Department to encourage people to enroll in an installment payment agreement; and
any additional information requested by the Director that is relevant to property tax arrearages and installment payment agreements authorized under this subtitle.

section.heading: Rules and regulations.
section.text: Subject to Title 4 {"Administrative Procedure Act – Regulations"} of the City General Provisions Article, the Director may adopt rules and regulations to carry out the provisions of this subtitle.

para.heading: Duties of Ombudsman.
para.text: The Ombudsman shall:
assist homeowners to understand the process for collection of delinquent taxes;
actively assist homeowners to apply for tax credits, discount programs, and other public benefits that may assist the homeowners to pay delinquent taxes and improve their financial situation;
refer homeowners to legal services, housing counseling, and other social services that may assist homeowners to pay delinquent taxes and improve their financial situation; and
maintain a website that:
functions as a clearinghouse for information concerning:
the process for collection of delinquent taxes; and
services and programs that are available to assist homeowners to pay delinquent taxes and improve their financial situation; and
includes:
a notice regarding eligibility to enroll in an installment payment plan;
a complete description of the installment payment programs established under Subtitles 7A and 7B of this article; and
detailed instructions on how to enroll in the installment payment programs established under Subtitles 7A and 7B of this article.
